title: helictochloa pratensis subsp. iberica
intro: Helictochloa pratensis subsp. iberica, also known as the Iberian meadow-grass, is a subspecies of grass native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Traditional herbal preparations of Helictochloa pratensis subsp. iberica include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive issues and promote overall well-being.
